rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EVELYN CECELIA OLSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/7/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) There is no attachment to the petition. (2) Who are the beneficiaries of the decedent's post-deceased brothers' estates? (3) The petition does not fully meet the requirements of LRSF 14.36. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/21/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] All pleadings must show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Return Of Trust Property, Financial Elder Abuse; Removal And Appointment Of Independednt Trustee; Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee, Imposition Of A Constructive Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/24/2025 to be calendared with related trust case (PTR-24-307887).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE PICHOTTO PROPERTY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 24,1993
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Ascertain Beneficiaries Of Trust, To Compel Trustee To Account, To Remove Trustee; And For An Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/24/2025 to be calendared with related trust case (PTR-24-307887). 1) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearane information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 30 days before the hearing. Direct notice must be given to each trustee and beneficiary, absent their directive under CRC 7.51(a)(2) that notice to their respective counsel is sufficient. 2) No proposed order has been received or lodged. A copy of the proposed order must be served on each person entitled to notice in a contested matter. Proof of service must be filed as a separate document (not as an attachment to the proposed order merely lodged). See LRSF 14.7(A)(4).

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ROBERT P. CLARKE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Substituted Judgment To: (1) Execute And Fund Restated Revocable Trust; (2) Execute Pour-Over Will; And (3) Authorization To Amend Retirement Account Beneficiary Designations
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) bond has not been addressed; 2) a copy of the proposed order was not served on opposing counsel. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEFFERY D. PEARL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/14/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) A petitioner seeking appointment as a court-appointed fiduciary must sign and verify his own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment to his petition. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/1/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HARRY JOSEPH BOBINEAUX
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/14/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of publication of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate has been filed. (3) No Receipt of Acknowledgment of the Duties and Liabilities has been filed. (4) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (5) Items 2b of the petition is incorrect if there is no will, and the petitioner seeks Letters of Administration. (6) If there are no assets (item 3 of the petition), why is an estate being opened? (7) Item 3c of the petition is incomplete. (8) Items 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. (9) Item 8 of the petition must show the relationship to the decedent of any person listed. (10) Neither the petition caption nor item 2c of the petition was completed to indicate that the petitioner seeks any IAEA authority It appears that an amended petition should be filed to set forth all of the correct and complete information. After the amended petition is filed, notice must be published. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the amended petition and any other papers to the assigned examiner by 4/1/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
